name: Irish Stew with Frozen Tofu
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour boiling water over the tofu, cover if possible, and leave for 10 to 15 minutes.
2. Chop the onion. Dice the potatoes and carrot into small pieces.
3. Add the yeast extract to the stock or water and bring to a boil in a saucepan.
4. Add the onions and potatoes, lower heat, and simmer for 7-10 minutes. Add the carrots and cook for an additional 4-5 minutes.
5. In a bowl, mix together the flour, salt, and baking powder. Cut in the margarine until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs. Stir in enough soymilk (about 2 tablespoons) to form a soft dough. Divide into four balls.
6. In a small bowl, combine the soy sauce, water, and vinegar.
7. Drain the tofu and gently squeeze out excess liquid. Dice the tofu. Add it to the bowl with the soy sauce mixture and stir until absorbed.
8. Dust the tofu cubes with flour on a plate. Heat oil in a skillet and stir-fry the tofu cubes until lightly browned.
9. Add the tofu cubes to the vegetable mixture. Transfer to an oven dish, flatten the dough balls on top, and place in a preheated 435F (220C) Gas Mark 7 oven for 15 minutes. Serve immediately.
